taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Data Analysis, Data Scientist
core_skills: Predictive Modeling, Machine Learning, Python, R, SQL
skill_match_scores: Predictive Modeling:0.5, Machine Learning:1.0, Python:1.0, R:0.0, SQL:1.0
must_have_quals: Bachelor's degree in a related field, 3+ years of experience building predictive models using machine learning methods, Expert proficiency in Python or R and advanced proficiency in SQL, Experience working with large, structured, and unstructured datasets, Experience building and working with Generative and Agentic AI
preferred_quals: Familiarity with GitHub, CI/CD pipelines, and MLOps best practices
core_responsibilities: Clean, preprocess, and validate data using SQL and Python/R to ensure model inputs are accurate and analysis-ready, Build predictive models using Python or R, applying machine learning, generative and agentic AI techniques, and advanced statistical techniques, Analyze course-level performance and identify key trends and equity gaps, Support experiment design and A/B testing frameworks that assess the impact of interventions and curriculum changes, Support model monitoring and evaluation efforts, tracking performance over time and flagging unexpected changes, Collaborate across engineering, product, and program development teams to support data-powered curriculum improvements, including assisting with pipeline development and documentation efforts, Communicate findings clearly with technical and non-technical stakeholders
job_summary: Western Governors University is dedicated to expanding access to higher education through innovative online programs. As a Data Scientist on the Learning Program Analytics team, you will leverage your technical skills to optimize curriculum and enhance learning outcomes for students nationwide.
